pending.body: finding_summary: `BrMutationAdapter::sync_import()` only checks the adapter's in-memory dirty flag, so every fresh adapter instance is treated as clean even after a prior process crashed with unflushed `br` state. `BrMutationAdapter::new()` / `with_adapter()` always initialize `has_unsync_mutations` to `false` (src/adapters/br_process.rs:496-516), and `sync_import()` relies exclusively on `ensure_synced()` before running `br sync --import-only` (src/adapters/br_process.rs:529-543, 713-724). That means the documented recovery flow after `git pull` / failed-run restart can still import upstream JSONL over stale local mutations, which defeats the safety guarantee this bead is adding. severity: high. affected_files: src/adapters/br_process.rs. remediation_hint: persist or reconstruct pending-mutation state across restarts before allowing `sync_import()`, or narrow the API so import is rejected unless the caller has independently proven the workspace is clean.

Missing test for `BrMutationAdapter::sync_import()` refusing to run when the adapter has pending (dirty) mutations. The doc comments and `ensure_synced` guard in `sync_import()` codify this as a key safety invariant, but no test exercises this rejection path. Add a test in `src/adapters/br_process.rs` that: (1) makes a mutation to set the dirty flag, (2) calls `sync_import()`, (3) asserts the call returns an error about pending mutations, and (4) verifies no subprocess was actually invoked via the command log.
